title: Shujing: Counsels of Yu the Great
source: Old Text and New Text recensions
description: From the Shujing (Book of Documents) — the foundational Chinese historical text, recording the speeches of legendary sage-kings.
introduction: From the Shujing (Book of Documents) — the foundational Chinese historical text, recording the speeches of legendary sage-kings.

translation.text: Examining into antiquity, we find that the great Yu was called Wen-ming. Having arranged and divided the empire, all to the four seas, in respectful response to the Emperor, he said: 'When the sovereign is virtuous, his government is good. The government depends on nourishing the people.'
